title: Tourbillion v0.1.1
content:
Tourbillion moved from a promising imported app into a cleaner first-maintainable release. This patch focused on turning the giant single-file render loop into a more durable structure without changing the public identity or the live static deployment model.

What changed:

  • Completed a major rendering split by moving the remaining visual modes out of App.tsx and into dedicated mode files for starfield, matrix, mystify, pipes, toasters, and trains.
  • Added shared initializer helpers so mode setup logic now has a clearer home instead of being mixed into the top-level app controller.
  • Reduced App.tsx from a giant all-purpose rendering surface into a coordinator that mainly handles state, resets, mode switching, and animation flow.
  • Kept the earlier quality controls in place, so the heavier train scenes still retain Low / Medium / High performance tuning while the internal structure gets cleaner.
  • Verified the refactor with a clean npm run build and npm run lint, making this a safe checkpoint for future visual tuning and mode-specific experimentation.
  • Documented the release in the shared cross-computer action trail so the current structure, build state, and next steps are easier to pick up from either machine.

The result is not a visual redesign yet; it is a stability and maintainability pass. Tourbillion now has a much better internal skeleton for the next phase of polish, interaction tuning, and mode-specific work.
